soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / finite-realization restriction
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked observable law P is the full law of (Y,A,W,Z,X) with Y in [0,1], but the finite response-type realization only specifies real potential-outcome coordinates and cannot induce an arbitrary continuous conditional law of Y; either restrict P to the finite mean law used by T_P and m_P or add an explicit residual/randomization component that reproduces the observed Y distribution.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Conjecture 1 by making the realized object the observed proxy/treatment/covariate law plus conditional outcome means, or by adding a finite-mixture/noise kernel that preserves the full observed Y law.

State a minimal nontrivial finite example for Conjecture 1 with explicit W,Z,A supports and a bridge-null direction, so the support-size bound is anchored by a concrete construction rather than only a Caratheodory route.

For Conjecture 2, add a boundary corollary comparing Gamma_P with the Bennett/Santos strong-functional condition: Gamma_P=0 under adjoint-range/null-annihilation and positive Gamma_P on the proposed one-dimensional active-singular open class.

recommended_next_step: Revise the realization statement so it matches the observable object actually used by the bridge LP, then keep the two conjectures as the flagship novelty kernel.
